$schema-note: Hoard capability parity map. Per-artifact registry of skills/agents and their counterpart on the other side of the pi/cc boundary. The stop-parity-check hook reads this file and warns when artifacts on disk are unregistered or when cross-references point at missing files. Update this map whenever you add a skill, agent, or extension. See cc-plugin/skills/parity-check/SKILL.md for the full schema and examples.

cross-ref-values.null: Intentionally unavailable on the other side (pi-only or cc-only capability).
cross-ref-values.cc-plugin:<name>: cc-plugin artifact at cc-plugin/skills/<name>/ or cc-plugin/agents/<name>.md — disk-checked.
cross-ref-values.cc-builtin:<name>: External CC skill/plugin (superpowers, skill-creator, simplify, defuddle, etc.) — informational only, not disk-checked.
cross-ref-values.pi-builtin:<name>: External pi capability — informational only, not disk-checked.

defuddle.note: pi uses the morsel, CC uses the installed defuddle skill

hoard-allies.note: pi dispatches via hoard-allies morsel; cc dispatches via quest skill + ally-* subagents

hoard-sending-stone.note: both talk to storybook-daemon stone, different surfaces

refactoring.note: for review-then-fix loops CC prefers cc-builtin:simplify — see description nudge

skill-designer.note: CC also has superpowers:writing-skills — skill-designer is pi-only

memory.note: persona memory is served by storybook-daemon MCP on both sides (mcp__storybook-ember__memory_*); no pi-side berrygem wraps it today

parity-check.note: cc-only audit skill for this map

ally-scout.note: pi uses a single hoard-allies berrygem with role-based dispatch; cc splits roles into discrete subagent files
